title: prumnopitys utilior
intro: Prumnopitys utilior, also known as the red pine or tāheke, is a species of coniferous tree native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the plant's high concentration of terpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Prumnopitys utilior are numerous, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. Prumnopitys utilior is often prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, and its leaves and bark are also used in traditional herbal preparations.
